steps.title: Understand when a linear equation has no solution
steps.content: For a linear equation in the form $ax = b$, there is no solution when $a = 0$ but $b \neq 0$—this creates a contradiction like $0 = 5$. So we rearrange the given equation into this form and determine when the coefficient of $x$ equals zero.

steps.title: Rearrange to isolate the coefficient of x
steps.content:
Starting with $-\frac{3}{19}rx+\frac{s}{8}=10-\frac{5}{57}x$, collect all $x$ terms on the left and constants on the right by adding $\frac{5}{57}x$ to both sides and subtracting $\frac{s}{8}$:
$$x\left(-\frac{3}{19}r + \frac{5}{57}\right) = 10 - \frac{s}{8}$$
The coefficient of $x$ is $-\frac{3}{19}r + \frac{5}{57}$.

steps.title: Set the coefficient to zero and solve for r
steps.content:
For no solution, set the coefficient equal to zero:
$$-\frac{3}{19}r + \frac{5}{57} = 0$$
$$\frac{3}{19}r = \frac{5}{57}$$
$$r = \frac{5}{57} \cdot \frac{19}{3} = \frac{95}{171}$$
Simplify by factoring the common factor of 19: $95 = 5 \cdot 19$ and $171 = 9 \cdot 19$, so:
$$r = \frac{5}{9}$$
